title: mentha aquatica subsp. crispa
intro: Mentha aquatica subsp. crispa, commonly known as watermint or curled watermint, is a subspecies of the water mint plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve headaches, and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica subsp. crispa include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
